title: Close the chain and prove the whole tree green
action: Per D18/D20 and PAT37. Earlier cells each owned their own regen, so this cell CONFIRMS rather than repairs — if it finds work to do, that is itself a finding worth reporting. Run the chain in order: (1) `node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs`; (2) `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write`; (3) `node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply`; (4) confirm .bee/bin/bee.mjs matches skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs byte for byte — scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs does NOT check that pair (scripts/test_native_probe.mjs:355-357). Then run the FULL verify and record its output verbatim. Measured baseline for comparison: 84 suites, exit 0, ~63.6s wall at concurrency 5 — if the suite count or wall-time moved materially, say so.

truths: `node scripts/run_verify.mjs` exits 0 with its output recorded verbatim — recorded, never asserted.
A fresh onboarding dry-run reports no drift.
Whether steps 1-3 produced any diff at all is reported explicitly: a non-empty diff means an earlier cell left its own regen undone, which is the finding this cell exists to surface.
artifacts: Regenerated plugin trees, release manifest, onboarding ledger, and the recorded full-verify output
prohibitions: Never hand-edit .bee/onboarding.json hashes — run the onboarding apply.
Do not cap on a partial verify; the whole suite is the proof.
Do not silently absorb a diff from steps 1-3 — report it.

trace.dropped_reason: Revision 2 failed validation (6 CRITICAL). Superseded by revision 3 under D23-D26: widened regen trigger, PreCompact row split, four-way count vocabulary, corrected handoffOutcome premise.
